topic: The Protein Timing Window Is a Myth

script.hook: You DON'T need to chug a protein shake within 30 minutes of your workout.
script.tension: The 'anabolic window' — the idea that you MUST eat protein right after training or your gains disappear — has been repeated so many times it feels like law. But science says otherwise.
script.explanation: A 2013 meta-analysis in the Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition looked at 23 studies. The result? Total daily protein intake matters way more than timing. Your body doesn't shut off muscle repair after 30 minutes. It stays elevated for 24 to 48 hours. The only people who might benefit from fast post-workout protein are those training completely fasted — and even then, the effect is small.
script.punchline: Stop stressing about the clock. Just hit your daily protein. Follow for more.

Your body doesn't shut off muscle repair after 30 minutes. It stays elevated for 24 to 48 hours. The only people who might benefit are those training completely fasted — and even then, the effect is small.
script_with_timings.20-28s_PUNCHLINE: Stop stressing about the clock. Just hit your daily protein. Follow for more.

youtube.title: You DON'T Need Protein Within 30 Minutes 💪
youtube.description:
Your body is smarter than you think.

The "anabolic window" — the idea that you must eat protein within 30 minutes of your workout — is one of the biggest myths in fitness. A meta-analysis of 23 studies found that total daily protein intake matters far more than timing. Your body keeps repairing muscle for 24-48 hours.

Follow @YourBodyExplainedHQ for daily science-backed fitness facts.

#shorts #fitness #protein #nutrition #gym #muscle #sciencebased #health #fyp

tiktok.description: The 30-minute protein window is a MYTH 🧬💪 Your body repairs muscle for 24-48 hours — just hit your daily protein. #fitness #protein #nutrition #gym #muscle #sciencebased #learnontiktok #bodyfacts #fyp
